A brief landspout was reported via numerous photos and videos. One photo in particular appears to confirm the circulation briefly touched the ground by kicking up dust in a construction area. NWS employee surveyed the approximate location and talked to several witnesses in the area, but could not locate any damage, and no damage was reported to the local police departments. Location/time approximate from reports and radar.

Public photograph of water over roadway and localized flooding near the intersection of 800 N and 1100 E at Meads Wash. The KPUC ASOS reported 0.44 inches of rain in less than 15 minutes, and then an additional 0.59 over the following half hour, for rainfall totaling 1.03 inches over roughly 45 minutes. MRMS rainfall estimates showed similar amounts near the flood area.

From Utah Division of Emergency Management: Flash flooding in eastern Garfield County caused 4 people to be stranded on an elevated rock in Hog Canyon near Highway 95. Department of Public Safety helicopter and Swift Water Rescue crews were on standby. Individuals were able to move safely to dry ground. (Time estimated from radar).

Time estimated from radar and reports. Heavy rain resulted in a flash flood off the Bear Burn Scar. Mitigation efforts in Crandall and Bear Canyons allowed for limited impacts. At the intersection of U.S. 6 and U.S. 191, there was a flood in the median because a drainage was clogged by debris. There was mud across U.S. 191 at mile post 272. A few small rocks made it to U.S 6 near Crandall and Bear Canyons. Drainage ditches were filled with debris.
Large section of tree roughly 12 inches or so in diameter split from trunk. Per pictures, this fell mostly into yard, but some minor gutter damage to house was reported. Time estimated by radar.

Updates previous flash flood report from Saratoga Springs. Per county official, homes in Saratoga Springs have sustained water damage due to intense bursts of rain in particular homes on South Island Road. City parks have also been impacted with floodwaters. Culverts have filled with water. Other areas around town where construction was ongoing were also impacted. Time estimated.

Per local official, homes in Saratoga Springs have sustained water damage due to intense bursts of rain. Culverts have filled with water. Other areas around town where construction was ongoing were also impacted. Time estimated.

Time and location are an estimate. A flood wave was observed at the lone gauge upstream on Muddy Creek near Emery, rising from around 34 CFS to around 700 CFS between 4:45 and 5PM MDT. This flood wave would have then routed into the warned area between 5 and 6PM MDT.
Flash flooding report at Catstair Canyon, time and location approximate.
Video showing flash flooding on the East Fork Virgin River near Barracks Ranch River Crossing. Shared by a professor of atmospheric science at the University Of Utah. Time and location approximate.
East Fork of the Virgin River showed two distinct flash flood waves. One at 845 PM MDT around 145 cfs and one at 1045 PM MDT around 500 cfs. Time on LSR is for the first wave.

Snow Canyon State Park reported flash flooding. Campers were displaced from the campsite, multiple waterfalls were observed, and employees had to wait on water to recede at a low water crossing as the flowing water was up to the car door handles.
Zion National Park reported multiple waterfalls and high flows in multiple slot canyons off of Zion Canyon.

The North Fork of the Virgin River spiked from 58 CFS to 251 CFS in just 5 minutes, peaking at 264 CFS within 10 minutes. Around this time Zion Park Rangers reported elevated flows and waterfalls in canyons continued.

Public reported property damage. Several large objects moved. Solar panel relocated. Time estimated by radar.

Wyoming DOT and the public reported Interstate 80 Westbound closure near milepost 13 due to multiple crashes and a pileup as a result of a snow squall. In the hour preceding the incidents WYDOT RWIS sensors at West Divide and Painter recorded heavy snow and visibility down to between one quarter and one eighth of a mile with an accompanying flash freeze. Gusty winds did not accompany these squalls. Time is approximate.
